vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: structural integrity under wing bending loads

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment during assembly due to lack of clear datum references.
risk.mit: Add primary datum features and ensure clear assembly sequence.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling if airflow is obstructed.
risk.mit: Ensure clear airflow path and verify with CFD.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under wing bending loads if reinforcement is inadequate.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and verify reinforcement adequacy.

risk.desc: Risk of environmental seal failure due to dust and humidity exposure.
risk.mit: Select appropriate seal materials and conduct environmental testing.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical connectivity issues if harness path is not properly routed.
risk.mit: Ensure proper routing and conduct signal integrity tests.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Equipment bay, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon

physics_paths.description: Transmits wing bending loads from root to motor mount
Airflow for motor cooling
Thermal management from sun exposure to internal structure
Door opening and closing motion
Data and power lines from sensors to motor pod

verification_focus.requirement: structural integrity under wing bending loads

verify.rule: Each internal_feature_target must be referenced in at least 4 geometry_ops.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to direct sun exposure.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as heat sinks or reflective coatings.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interfaces.
risk.mit: Use precise alignment tools and detailed assembly instructions.

risk.desc: Risk of over-tightening bolts during assembly.
risk.mit: Provide torque wrenches with pre-set values for each fastener.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.path: Lift force from wing to fuselage frames via spar, ribs, and carry-through
Airflow over wing surface, through ribs and channels
Heat from sun to wing spar, dissipated through CFRP
Wing bending and torsion loads transferred through anti-rotation key
Wiring harness from wing spar to fuselage electronics

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: visual and tactile inspection for fit and finish

generated_parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
generated_parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

requirements.description: Support wing bending loads from spar to leading edge
Provide airflow path through lightening pocket to trailing edge
Dissipate heat from winglet to elevon
Enable control surface movement around hinge line
Connect sensor bay to service access port for electrical/data
Provide service access for sensor module installation

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion mismatch between EPP foam core and CFRP spar
risk.mit: Conduct thermal expansion analysis and select compatible materials

risk.desc: Risk of control surface flutter due to unbalanced aerodynamic forces
risk.mit: Perform aerodynamic analysis and adjust control surface design as needed

risk.desc: Potential for fatigue failure at spar/socket interface under repeated load cycles
risk.mit: Include fatigue analysis and reinforce critical areas as necessary

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress through service access ports in humid environments
risk.mit: Design service access ports with appropriate seals and drainage features

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment during assembly due to lack of clear assembly sequence

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing planform, airfoil section evidence, winglets, elevon/control surface cutouts with hinges

physics_paths.description: Transfers wing bending loads from spar to leading edge
Airflow path through lightening pocket to trailing edge
Heat dissipation from winglet to elevon
Control surface movement around hinge line
Electrical and data connection from sensor bay to service access port
Service access path for sensor module installation

integration_opportunities.description: Integration of spar socket with wing spar for load transfer
Integration of lightening pocket with airfoil section for weight reduction
Integration of control surface cutout with hinge line for control surface movement

verification_focus.description: Verify spar socket and leading edge sock for load transfer
Verify airfoil section and lightening pocket for aerodynamic performance
Verify control surface cutout and hinge line for control surface movement

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to heat dissipation from servo and control horn.
risk.mit: Implement heat sinks and thermal management strategies to mitigate thermal stress.

risk.desc: Risk of cable strain during assembly and operation.
risk.mit: Ensure strain relief geometry is present at every cable exit and routing respects minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating connectors due to lack of keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Add keying or labeling to connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly sequence ambiguity.
risk.mit: Define a clear assembly sequence with tool, torque, and datum information.

risk.desc: Risk of dimensional inaccuracy due to placeholder dimensions.
risk.mit: Ensure all dimensions are physically plausible and match the subsystem scale.

physics_paths.path: MG90S servo actuates control surface via control horn and pushrod
Control horn linkage and pushrod channel allow linear motion
Heat from servo and control horn dissipates through EPP foam core
Access cover provides entry for maintenance and inspection
Electrical harness connects servo to control board

integration_opportunities.description: Servo pocket integrates MG90S or DS215MG servo
Control horn provides mechanical linkage to control surface
Pushrod channel guides control horn linkage
Control surface cutout allows control surface to articulate
Removable access cover provides access for maintenance

risk.desc: Insufficient cooling air flow rate may cause motor overheating
risk.mit: Increase cooling inlet size or add additional cooling channels

risk.desc: Propeller clearance may be insufficient for folding prop
risk.mit: Adjust propeller clearance dimensions to accommodate folding prop

risk.desc: Fastener locking method may not be adequate for high vibration environment
risk.mit: Use locknuts or adhesive to ensure fastener integrity

risk.desc: Structural reinforcement may not be sufficient for wing bending loads
risk.mit: Increase wall thickness or add additional stiffeners

risk.desc: Seal may not be effective in dust and humidity environment
risk.mit: Use dust-proof seals and apply appropriate sealant

gps signal from sky to avionics tray
pitot static pressure from air to avionics tray
airflow around pitot boom
human access for maintenance

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex fastener pattern.
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly sequence with torque specifications.

risk.desc: Thermal dissipation may be compromised if heat sink is not properly aligned.
risk.mit: Include alignment pins and visual indicators for heat sink installation.

generated_parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
generated_parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

requirements: Provide a slide-in bay for a 4S 16000mAh Li-ion battery pack
Include BMS and connector access points
Ensure CG adjustment marks are visible
Top access hatch for balance lead monitoring
Maintain structural integrity and thermal dissipation
Ensure service access and cable harness visibility

verify.rule: All required fields and features are present.
Mass is within the expected range for the subsystem.
All P0 internal_feature_targets are represented in the geometry_ops.
All interfaces have reciprocal definitions in adjacent subsystems.
Visible locking/preload evidence is present for critical joints.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to heat dissipation from the battery pack.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management solutions such as heat sinks and cooling channels.

risk.desc: Risk of connector mis-mating due to lack of keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Ensure all connectors have unique keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Potential strain on cables due to unsupported routing.
risk.mit: Add strain relief geometry at every cable exit and ensure routing respects minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to load path discontinuity.
risk.mit: Ensure continuous structural load paths from spar to fuselage frames.

risk.desc: Potential difficulty in assembly due to ambiguous build sequence.
risk.mit: Define a clear assembly sequence with tool, torque, and datum for each step.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Slide-in li-ion battery pack bay on CG rail, BMS and connector access

physics_paths.path_description: Power is supplied from the battery pack to the electronic control unit through XT60 connector
Heat generated by the battery pack is dissipated to the wing structure through conduction and natural convection
Service access is provided through a top access hatch to balance leads for maintenance and monitoring
Structural loads from the wing are transferred to the battery bay shell through the CG rail and mounting interfaces
Linear motion is provided by the slide rail for battery pack insertion and removal

physics_paths.path: From wing spar to fuselage bulkhead via anti-rotation datum and harness raceway
Service fluids and electrical harnesses from wing root to wingtip
Heat dissipation from electronics to wing surface via harness raceway
Wing flexure and rotation around anti-rotation datum
Pressure equalization through service hatch
Data and power lines from wing sensors to fuselage avionics
Field service access through service hatch
